en: And Job again took up his discourse, and said:
zh: 約伯繼續他的講論，說：

en: "Oh, that I were as in the months of old, as in the days when God watched over me,
zh: “但願我的景況像以前的歲月， 像 神保守我的日子。

en: when his lamp shone upon my head, and by his light I walked through darkness,
zh: 那時他的燈照在我的頭上， 我靠著他的光行過黑暗。

en: as I was in my prime, when the friendship of God was upon my tent,
zh: 但願我仍然活在年輕力壯的日子， 那時 神在我的帳棚裡與我親密。

en: when the Almighty was yet with me, when my children were all around me,
zh: 全能者仍然與我同在， 我的孩子都環繞我。

en: when my steps were washed with butter, and the rock poured out for me streams of oil!
zh: 我用油洗腳， 磐石為我出油成河。

en: When I went out to the gate of the city, when I prepared my seat in the square,
zh: 我出到城門那裡去， 在廣場上預備我的座位。

en: the young men saw me and withdrew, and the aged rose and stood;
zh: 年輕人看見我，就迴避， 年老的也起身站立。

en: the princes refrained from talking and laid their hand on their mouth;
zh: 王子都停止說話， 並且用手掩口。

en: the voice of the nobles was hushed, and their tongue stuck to the roof of their mouth.
zh: 眾領袖都不敢作聲， 他們的舌頭緊貼上顎。

en: When the ear heard, it called me blessed, and when the eye saw, it approved,
zh: 耳朵聽見我的，就稱我有福。 眼睛看見我的，就作證稱讚我。

en: because I delivered the poor who cried for help, and the fatherless who had none to help him.
zh: 因為我救了呼救的窮人， 和無人幫助的孤兒。

en: The blessing of him who was about to perish came upon me, and I caused the widow's heart to sing for joy.
zh: 將要滅亡的，為我祝福； 我使寡婦的心歡呼。

en: I put on righteousness, and it clothed me; my justice was like a robe and a turban.
zh: 我以公義作衣服穿上； 我的公平好像外袍和冠冕。

en: I was eyes to the blind and feet to the lame.
zh: 我作了瞎子的眼， 瘸子的腿。

en: I was a father to the needy, and I searched out the cause of him whom I did not know.
zh: 我作過貧窮人的父親， 我查究過我素來不認識的人的案件。

en: I broke the fangs of the unrighteous and made him drop his prey from his teeth.
zh: 我打碎了不義的人的牙齒， 使捕食的掉下來。

en: Then I thought, 'I shall die in my nest, and I shall multiply my days as the sand,
zh: 那時我心裡想：‘我必在家中安然去世， 我必增添我的日子像沙塵那麼多。

en: my roots spread out to the waters, with the dew all night on my branches,
zh: 我的根蔓延到水邊， 露水整夜沾在我的枝上。

en: my glory fresh with me, and my bow ever new in my hand.'
zh: 我的榮耀在身上常新， 我的弓在我手上重新得力。

en: "Men listened to me and waited and kept silence for my counsel.
zh: 大家都聆聽我，等候我； 靜默無聲地領受我的指導。

en: After I spoke they did not speak again, and my word dropped upon them.
zh: 我說了話以後，他們就不再說， 我的言語像雨露一般滴在他們身上。

en: They waited for me as for the rain, and they opened their mouths as for the spring rain.
zh: 他們等候我像等候雨水， 又大大張嘴如切慕春雨。

en: I smiled on them when they had no confidence, and the light of my face they did not cast down.
zh: 我向他們微笑，他們也不敢相信； 他們珍惜我臉上的光。

en: I chose their way and sat as chief, and I lived like a king among his troops, like one who comforts mourners.
zh: 我為他們選擇道路，又坐首位； 我像君王住在軍隊中， 又像個安慰悲傷的人。’”
